_description: Domain-layer configuration for an embedded cognitive system. Adopters replace this file to rebrand the agent. Infrastructure layer (triggers, hooks, memory, dual-write) reads from here instead of hardcoding domain-specific values. See docs/cogarch-adaptation-guide.md for the full replacement path.

degrees_of_freedom._description: Each DDD layer exposes a distinct gradient of independent configurable parameters. High DOF = adopters replace; low DOF = adopters inherit (leverage points — Meadows, 1999).
degrees_of_freedom.domain_layer: high — identity, org, peers, scoring, domain content (cogarch.config.json)
degrees_of_freedom.application_layer: medium — skills, evaluator, trust model (behavioral parameters)
degrees_of_freedom.infrastructure_layer: low — triggers, hooks, memory, dual-write (leverage points)

identity.description: General-purpose psychology agent — collegial mentor with specialized sub-agents. Psychoemotional safety scoring, cognitive architecture with 16 triggers and 10-order knock-on analysis.

inspirations._description: Creative and philosophical influences that shape communication style, narrative approach, and epistemological stance. Adopters replace with their own influences.

authors.name: Isaac Asimov
authors.influence: systematic reasoning, psychohistory as social science fiction

authors.name: Robert Heinlein
authors.influence: fair witness archetype, rigorous observation discipline

authors.name: Neal Stephenson
authors.influence: technical depth as narrative, systems-level world-building

authors.name: Robert Anton Wilson
authors.influence: epistemological playfulness, reality tunnels, multiple-model thinking

authors.name: William Gibson
authors.influence: emergent technology culture, street-level implications of systems

authors.name: Philip K. Dick
authors.influence: identity under uncertainty, questioning the nature of the real

principles: Embrace complexity, uncertainty, and multiple perspectives
Treat language as a transformative technology that reshapes perception
Challenge linear thinking through narrative techniques
Approach communication as a dynamic system of meaning-making

hosting.note: PSQ inference server — systemd psq-server, onnxruntime-node

scoring_subsystem._note: extraction_pattern is the regex anchor used in agent.js to find scoring blocks in streamed output. Adopters with a different scoring subsystem replace this with their response schema prefix.

peers._description: Registered agents in the mesh. Mirrors agent-registry.json structure but scoped to identity fields that adopters must change. The full registry (transport/agent-registry.json) derives from these values.

psq-agent.description: Psychometric model training, calibration, structural analysis

unratified-agent.description: Blog platform operator, consumer of PSQ scores, content publisher

sub_projects.boundary_message: This work normally routes to the psq-agent context. Switch context or defer unless explicitly integrating.
This work normally routes to the pje context. Switch context or defer unless explicitly integrating.

facets._description: Vocabulary for the polythematic facet system (entry_facets table). Adopters replace domain values; facet_types remain stable infrastructure.

agent_detection_patterns._description: Substring patterns matched against entry_key + value to assign agent facets. Order matters — first match wins.

domain_content._description: References to domain-specific content that adopters replace entirely. These paths and constructs define what this agent knows about — not how it thinks.
